records.ety: [OE. emmeres, emeres, AS. <?/myrie; akin to Icel. eimyrja, Dan. emmer, MHG. eimere; cf. Icel. eimr vapor, smoke.]

defs: A lighted coal, smoldering amid ashes; -- used chiefly in the plural, to signify mingled coals and ashes; the smoldering remains of a fire.
quotes: He takes a lighted ember out of the covered vessel.

records.ety: [OE. ymber, AS. ymbren, ymbryne, prop., running around, circuit; ymbe around + ryne a running, fr. rinnan to run. See Amb-, and Run.]

defs: Making a circuit of the year of the seasons; recurring in each quarter of the year; as, ember fasts.
